summary: Backend testing of the new flat-schema Blog Webhook (POST /api/webhooks/blog) and supporting read endpoints. Created /app/backend/tests/test_blog_webhook.py with 12 pytest test cases covering: webhook create (EN+ES), idempotent update by id (created=false), invalid-slug 400, missing-field 422, list endpoint (with heavy-field exclusion + language filter EN/ES), detail endpoint (full payload + snake_case mapping + 404), and XML sitemap (structure, content-type, EN under /blog/ and ES under /es/blog/, lastmod). All 12 tests PASSED on first run against the public K8s ingress URL.

minor.issue: Endpoint is fully unauthenticated/unsigned - any caller on the internet can publish or overwrite blog articles. Recommend a shared-secret header (e.g. X-Webhook-Secret) verified server-side.
json_ld / faq_json_ld accept arbitrary nested JSON with extra='allow' on the model - no size cap. A malicious sender could store huge documents. Consider a max body-size guard.

Mongo find().limit(min(limit, 100)).to_list(100) ignores the smaller user-provided limit when converting to list (always materialises up to 100). Minor, but limit parameter is partially honoured only at cursor level.

URLs/lastmod values are interpolated into XML without escaping. If a slug ever contained `&`, `<` or `>`, the sitemap would become invalid XML. Slugs are sanitized to [a-z0-9-] so currently safe, but lastmod comes straight from the webhook payload and is not validated/escaped.

action_items: (optional) Add webhook authentication (shared secret header) to POST /api/webhooks/blog before exposing publicly.
(optional) XML-escape lastmod / loc values in /api/sitemap-blog.xml to harden against malformed payloads.
(optional) Honour the user-supplied limit in /api/blog-articles list (currently capped to 100 even when caller asks for less in to_list).
critical_code_review_comments: blog_webhook.py: Pydantic model uses extra='allow', so unknown fields are silently kept on the request object - fine for forward-compat but means malformed clients won't get clear errors.
Upsert key falls back to slug when external_id is missing, but external_id is always set to str(payload.id) and `id` is required by the model, so the fallback branch is effectively dead code.
sanitize_slug + SLUG_PATTERN combination correctly rejects empty/non-kebab-case slugs (verified by test_invalid_slug_returns_400).
List endpoint correctly excludes content_html/content_markdown/_id/source_ip for performance (verified).
Detail endpoint correctly returns snake_case fields (hero_image_url, json_ld, faq_json_ld, language_code, public_url) (verified).
seed_data_creation: Tests inserted 2 transient articles per run (slugs prefixed test-en-article-<run_tag> / test-es-articulo-<run_tag>) and cleaned them up via direct Mongo after the run. Pre-existing id=10 (English) and id=11 (Spanish 'como-reservar-traslado-paris') were preserved untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_blog_webhook.py uses unique per-run slugs derived from uuid so it can be re-run safely without collisions. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Pre-existing demo articles (id=10 English, id=11 Spanish slug 'como-reservar-traslado-paris') still exist in DB.
